q: Cocaine produces its effects by which mechanism at synapses?
options: It mimics inhibitory neurotransmitters, hyperpolarizing postsynaptic neurons
It destroys acetylcholinesterase, prolonging ACh activity at the neuromuscular junction
It blocks the reuptake of dopamine (and norepinephrine) into the presynaptic neuron, prolonging their effects in the cleft
It opens voltage-gated Na+ channels permanently, causing continuous action potentials
explanation: Cocaine blocks monoamine reuptake transporters, preventing dopamine and norepinephrine from being cleared from the synapse, amplifying and prolonging their stimulatory effects.
